该固件可快速体验Blynk同时通过串口通讯让任意单片机接入Blynk，利用固件板: This firmware can quickly experience Blynk and allow any microcontroller to access Blynk through serial communication. Using the onboard function of the firmware, you can also directly use Blynk to control the ATOM pin level status and obtain pin level changes. It can easily convert the sensor into an IoT remote sensor or actuator, making the use of the IoT more convenient. Simple, press and hold the onboard button for the first time without releasing it, click the reset button, release it when the indicator light flashes red and blue alternately, the device automatically turns on the hotspot ATOM_Blynk_XXXXXXXXX, password XXXXXXXX, connect to this hotspot to access the IP address 192.168.4.1 to access the configuration server and network After confirming that the network information is correct, restart the device and start using it. The device is disconnected from the Blynk server. The indicator light continues to flash with a cycle of 100 milliseconds. The baud rate of the hardware serial port and the soft serial port are both 9600, of which the soft serial port RX (26) TX (32), the serial port passes the jso of ["PIN", "value"] n format data interactive virtual pin data, for example ["V6", "1"] means that virtual pin V6 has sent or received data 1, V0-V7 has been occupied by ATOM. Please use other virtual pins for other purposes. The example server address is the domestic free Blynk server address for testing only. Please deploy the server privately for stable use. Warm reminder: When connecting to a computer for the first time, open the serial port monitor with a baud rate of 9600 and check the debugging information.

通过订阅MQTT主题改变板载指示灯颜色，同时单击,双击,长按开始,长按中与长按结:
Change the color of the onboard indicator light by subscribing to the MQTT topic, and simultaneously click, double-click, long press to start, long press to end, and long press to end to send 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 to the specified topic respectively. Use M5BurnerNVS to check and configure the following information:
ssid WiFi name
pass WiFi password
broker MQTT server address (default is public EMQX server address
broker.emqx.io）
username MQTT username (default is empty)
password MQTT user password (default is empty)
port MQTT open port (default is 1883)
client_id MQTT client ID (must be unique, if empty it will be automatically set to the device MAC address)
topic ATOM subscription topic (if it is empty, the subscription topic is IN_(MAC))
button ATOM onboard button sends the topic (if it is empty, the publishing topic is OUT_(MAC))

Note: The color of the ATOM onboard indicator light is in hexadecimal GRB mode, such as 0x00ff00 red, 0x0000f0 blue, 0xff0000 green, and others are analogous. When either the WiFi name or password is empty, the indicator light will flash 10 times in red, blue, and green order at intervals of 500 milliseconds to prompt for network configuration. Press and hold the onboard button without releasing it. Click the reset button. The onboard indicator light flashes red and black 20 times at an interval of 100 milliseconds. If you continue to press it, the WiFi information will be cleared and the network will be reconfigured.

A multi-function reader specially designed for M5Paper S3, supporting EPUB/TXT/pictures, with a simple interface and focused reading experience.

【Main functions】
- EPUB reading: supports table of contents, continued reading, comic mode, cover display
- TXT novel reading: automatic paging, support for large files
- Picture browsing and file management
- WiFi networking + SNTP automatic time adjustment
- Clock display
- Custom font rendering (BinFont), supports pre-baked acceleration

[Instructions for flashing]
1. Use m5Burner to directly burn and merge the firmware without manual partitioning.
2. An SD card is required, and the font files are placed in the root directory of the SD card (same directory structure as EDCBook)
3. Support the same .bin font file as EDCBook

【Basic operation】
- Click the cover/middle section on the home page to continue reading, click "Read" to enter the bookshelf, and click "File" to browse the SD card
- EPUB: Left/right click to turn pages, middle click to call out the menu
- If EPUB cannot be opened, delete booklet/epub/state and try again

PianDu v1.0 — E-Ink Reader Firmware for M5Paper S3

A full-featured e-ink reader for M5Paper S3, supporting EPUB, TXT, and image viewing.

[Features]
- EPUB reader with TOC, bookmarks, comic mode, and cover display
- TXT novel reader with auto-pagination for large files
-Image viewer and file manager
- WiFi + SNTP auto time sync
- Clock display
- Custom BinFont renderer with pre-baking acceleration

[Flash Instructions]
1. Flash merged_firmware.bin directly via m5Burner (no manual partitioning needed)
2. Requires SD card; place font .bin files in SD root (same layout as EDCBook)
3. Compatible with EDCBook font files out of the box

[Usage]
- Tap cover/center to resume reading; tap "Read" for bookshelf; tap "File" for SD
- EPUB: tap left/right to turn pages, tap center to open menu
- If an EPUB fails to open, delete booklet/epub/state and retry

The brightness of the clock and the bitmap showing 32x8

state (define the display time or display bitmap, the value is 1 to display the time, the value is 0 to display the bitmap)
image (displays the hexadecimal data of the bitmap, the height must be 8 and the width 0-32 of the bitmap, the state parameter of the displayed bitmap must be 0)
luminance (display brightness range 0-10, 0 is the darkest instead of not bright)

「オルゴール工房」\n手作り手回しオルゴールを楽しめるアプリです。\n手回しオル:
"Music box workshop"
This is an app where you can enjoy handmade hand-cranked music boxes.
You can enjoy the feeling of punching holes in the paper of a hand-cranked music box and playing it.
In real life, you would need many sheets of paper, but with this app, even if you make a mistake, you can correct it and try again as many times as you like.
You can write your own songs on the music box board and give them as gifts, or you can import them to your smartphone using a QR code.

Main features
- Song data can be transferred to the board and played on the board
- Can input from quarter note to 64th note
・256 steps for quarter notes, 4096 steps for 64th notes
- Possible to set tempo during playback
・2 octave keyboard input
・Simultaneous pronunciation of up to 3 notes per step
・Up to 250 sounds can be input
・Save data (automatically load when restarting the app)
・You can exchange composition data with your smartphone via QR code.
・Can save and read from SD card
・You can delete the input sound by reselecting it.
- Automatically scrolls as you type (when scrolling is on)
・Screen up/down scroll key (long press to fast forward)
-You can set the end position of the song using the END button.
・One line insertion and one line deletion function
・You can transfer the song data you created to the Frisk-sized music box workshop board and play it on the board.

How to use
●Top screen
When you press a key, that note will sound and a mark will be added.
The scroll will advance one note according to the set note (quarter note to 64th note).
Notes can be changed using the "↑" and "↓" buttons on the right.
You can move the editing position using the "↑" and "↓" buttons on the left, and the sound at the moved position will be played.
Press and hold the "↑" and "↓" buttons to fast forward. At this time, no sound will be played.
When you press the "Save" button, it will be saved to the device and automatically loaded the next time it is started.
To listen to the song you typed from the beginning, press the "Start" button to move to the beginning of the song, then press the "Play" button.
The performance will stop automatically when it reaches the end of the song.
If you want to stop midway through, press the "Play" button.
When you press the "Insert" button, one line (the length of the current note) is inserted at the current editing position.
Press the "Delete" button to delete one line at the current editing position.
Press the "Settings" button to display the settings menu screen.

●Settings menu screen
Pressing the "Delete All" button twice will clear all composition data.
If you press the "Free Performance" button to turn it on, you can enjoy playing freely without recording.
You can save data to the SD card using the "SD Save" button.
The file name to be saved can be an 8-digit number.
You can read song data saved on the SD card using the "SD Read" button.
Song data is limited to 100 songs.
If you turn on the "Auto Scroll" button, it will automatically scroll by one note when you press a key.
You can adjust the volume with the "Volume" button.
You can change the tempo with the "Tempo" button. (30-240)
When you press the "QR display" button, the current song data will be converted into a QR code and displayed.
You can transfer the composition data to the Music Box Studio board using the "Transfer" button.
It can be loaded using the smartphone app "Music Box Workshop".

●Transfer created song data to the board
You can transfer song data created to the music box workshop board and play it on the board.
Press the "Transfer" button on the menu to display the song data transfer screen.
Press and hold the button on the board to make the LED blink slowly (write mode).
Place the board so that the light sensor is directly above the light emitting part and press the "Transfer" button.
During writing, the LED on the board changes from flashing to lit, and the progress bar advances.
When data writing is successful, the LED on the board turns off.
If data transfer fails, the LED on the board will flash rapidly, so press the button on the board (to cancel write mode) and try again.

For videos on how to use it, please search for "Music Box Workshop" and "Writing song data to electronic music box board using smartphone app" on YouTube.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gKYO2o_FNh4
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EFtbN6PRWyo
The board is on sale at Mercari and Switch Science. Please search for "Music box workshop".

This system runs Hamlib on a Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W and exposes control functions through FastAPI.
Currently, the confirmed working configuration consists of:

Client: M5Core2 with Audio Module (M144)

Server: Raspberry Pi Zero 2W

Transceivers: Yaesu FT‑991A / Icom IC‑705

Hardware Extensions
PTT via Port B (Mechanical Key + SK6812):
A mechanical key connected to Port B can now trigger PTT.
When combined with a radio microphone or remote microphone, this enables convenient phone operation in a home remote‑shack environment.

Rotary Encoder on Port A (SKU: U135):
A rotary encoder connected to Port A can be used to operate the main screen controls.

Audio Reception via Module Audio (SKU: M144):
The system can receive audio from the transceiver through the M144 audio module.

Raspberry Pi Zero 2W Setup
For instructions on how to set up the Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W, please refer to the documentation on GitHub.
